display.purpose: Page headings and large KPI numeric values only.

body.purpose: All dense UI text, body copy, buttons, form fields.

label.purpose: Small caption text above KPI values and metadata tags.

shadows.name: card
shadows.purpose: Default resting shadow on cards; barely visible, grounding.

shadows.name: glow
shadows.purpose: Brass glow under primary buttons; the one place shadow carries brand color.

motion.name: ease-entrance
motion.purpose: Fade-up entrance animation for content reveals, staggered across list/grid children.

motion.name: duration-state
motion.purpose: Default transition duration for hover/focus state changes (background, border, box-shadow).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main call-to-action button, brass background with glow shadow.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; padding: 9px 17px; border-radius: 10px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; border: 1px solid transparent; cursor: pointer; background: #d9a05b; color: #1a1512; box-shadow: 0 10px 30px rgba(217,160,91,0.18); transition: transform 0.12s ease, background-color 0.18s ease, border-color 0.18s ease, box-shadow 0.18s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #e7b06a; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #d9a05b; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Secondary action button, card background with hairline border.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; padding: 9px 17px; border-radius: 10px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; background: #1c1815; color: #f2ebdd; border: 1px solid #312a22; transition: border-color 0.18s ease, background-color 0.18s ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: #d9a05b; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #d9a05b; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Filter Chip
components.description: Toggleable filter chip, active and inactive states.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-height: 36px; padding: 5px 13px; border-radius: 999px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; background: transparent; color: #a89a85; border: 1px solid #312a22; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.18s ease; } .ds-chip--active { font-weight: 600; background: rgba(217,160,91,0.14); color: #e7b06a; border-color: #d9a05b; } .ds-chip:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #d9a05b; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: KPI Tile
components.description: Clickable KPI tile with serif numeral, lifts and glows brass border on hover.
components.css: .ds-kpi { background: #1c1815; border: 1px solid #312a22; border-radius: 10px; padding: 15px 20px; min-width: 80px; flex: 1 1 0; cursor: pointer; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.4); transition: background-color 0.18s ease, border-color 0.18s ease, transform 0.18s ease; } .ds-kpi:hover { background: #262019; border-color: #d9a05b; transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-kpi__label { font-size: 11px; color: #a89a85; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; margin-bottom: 8px; } .ds-kpi__value { font-family: Fraunces, Georgia, serif; font-size: clamp(20px, 5vw, 32px); font-weight: 600; color: #f2ebdd; line-height: 1.05; }

components.name: Toast
components.description: Auto-dismissing notification with accent-colored border and dot, raised shadow.
components.css: .ds-toast { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; padding: 12px 16px; border-radius: 10px; background: #1c1815; border: 1px solid #8aa46a; color: #f2ebdd; font-size: 14px; box-shadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.4); min-width: 260px; max-width: 380px; } .ds-toast__dot { width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 50%; background: #8aa46a; flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Badge
components.description: Status/category pill with dimmed accent background.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 2px 9px; border-radius: 999px; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.02em; background: rgba(217,160,91,0.16); color: #d9a05b; border: 1px solid #d9a05b33; }

narrative.overview: VinylVault is built for a small group of personal collectors logging and browsing their own vinyl, not for an anonymous SaaS audience. The system reads like an amber-lit listening room at night: a near-black charcoal base, warm brass/amber as the one dominant accent, terracotta and olive and dusty teal as quiet supporting colors, and a faint film-grain texture across every screen that signals analog media rather than a flat productivity tool. Typography pairs a serif display face for warmth and character with a clean grotesque for body text, so headings feel personal and curated while data stays legible. This system explicitly rejects the generic SaaS dashboard: no gray flat cards, no gradient hero-metric tiles, no sterile enterprise-admin chrome. Depth and warmth come from tone, texture, and a single confident accent color, not from decoration.
keyCharacteristics: Dark, warm-neutral base (#141110) with brass/amber (#d9a05b) as the sole dominant accent
Subtle film-grain texture and soft radial glows for analog depth, never decorative gradients
Fraunces serif for display, Hanken Grotesk for body and UI text
Flat cards with hairline borders at rest; glow and lift only on hover/emphasis
Stable owner-color hashing (red/blue/teal/olive) so each collector's records are recognizable at a glance without hardcoded names

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Brass amber is the only color allowed to dominate a screen. Terracotta, teal, and olive exist only as small, purposeful signals — never as a competing primary.

rules.name: The Dim-Tint Rule
rules.body: When an accent needs a background instead of just a foreground, use that accent at low opacity (rgba(..., 0.14-0.16)) over the dark base rather than a flat saturated fill.

rules.name: The Serif-for-Numbers Rule
rules.body: Display/serif typography is reserved for headings and large numeric values in KPI tiles. Body copy, buttons, and form fields stay in Hanken Grotesk.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest, distinguished by tone, not shadow. Shadow is reserved for hover lift, the primary-button glow, and genuinely floating elements.

dos: Do keep brass amber (#d9a05b) as the only color allowed to dominate a screen.
Do use tone steps (bg-primary -> bg-card -> bg-hover) to convey depth before reaching for shadow.
Do pair Fraunces only with headings and large numeric values; keep dense UI text in Hanken Grotesk.
Do use the dimmed-accent-background pattern (rgba(accent, 0.14-0.16)) for badges and active filter chips instead of flat saturated fills.
Do keep the deterministic owner-color hash for any new per-person UI; never hardcode a person's color.
donts: Don't introduce gray flat cards or sterile enterprise-admin styling; this is the explicit anti-reference from PRODUCT.md.
Don't use gradient hero-metric tiles or gradient text for KPI values; the serif numeral plus flat brass tint already carries emphasis.
Don't add ambient shadows to every card 'for depth'; depth comes from background tone, shadow is reserved for hover/floating states.
Don't introduce a second saturated accent competing with brass amber for visual weight.
Don't strip the film-grain texture or warm radial glow from the page background; it's the load-bearing detail that keeps the dark theme from feeling like generic SaaS dark mode.
